mission.title: Install and Configure Redpanda
mission.description: Redpanda is a high-performance streaming data platform that is compatible with the Kafka API. It is designed to be faster and simpler to operate without the need for ZooKeeper or JVMs. Ideal for developers looking for a lightweight and efficient messaging solution.

steps.title: Add Redpanda Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redpanda Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redpanda https://charts.redpanda.com

```

> This is the official repository for Redpanda Helm charts.

steps.title: Install Redpanda CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Redpanda to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/crds/redpanda.vectorized.io_redpandas.yaml

```

steps.title: Install Redpanda using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Redpanda in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --create-namespace --version 25.3.10

```

steps.title: Create a Redpanda Cluster Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) for Redpanda to define the cluster configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-redpanda
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
  configuration:
    auto_create_topics_enabled: true
EOF

```
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-redpanda
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
  configuration:
    auto_create_topics_enabled: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify Redpanda Cluster Status
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Redpanda cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda

```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Redpanda
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redpanda installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall redpanda --namespace redpanda

kubectl delete namespace redpanda

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Redpanda
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade Redpanda to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --version 25.3.10

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node being upgraded.
> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node that was drained.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Redpanda pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust the configuration or resources accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Redpanda cluster may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Verify the Custom Resource definition and ensure it was applied correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** RBAC settings may not allow the necessary permissions for Redpanda.

**Fix:**
Check and update the Role and RoleBinding configurations to grant the required permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance for topic partitioning and enhanced metrics reporting.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from 25.x.

resolution.summary: You should see the Redpanda cluster listed with 'Running' status when executing 'kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda'.

metadata:

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
